name: Puerto de Casielles – round trip from the Área recreativa Trambesagües
name_original: Puerto de Casielles – ida y vuelta desde el Área recreativa Trambesagües
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Amieva, located in the Principality of Asturias, traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the rugged Picos de Europa National Park. The region features a mix of quiet roads, varied elevation profiles, and excellent road surfaces, including recently paved sections. Cyclists can expect routes that lead through mixed deciduous forests, ascend to higher karstic elevations, and descend into green river valleys. Best road cycling routes around Amieva The most popular road cycling route is Trail to the Lakes of Covadonga, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route offers significant climbs and views towards the iconic Lakes of Covadonga. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Collada Llomena and Amieva Loop, a difficult 31.2 miles (50.3 km) path. This loop features substantial elevation gain, passing through varied terrain and offering panoramic views of the Asturian landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís, a 51.3 miles (82.6 km) trail leading through the Picos de Europa National Park, often completed in about 4 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Amieva is defined by the dramatic Picos de Europa mountains, dense forests, and scenic river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents. The routes in Amieva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 5,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Amieva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Amieva?
A: Amieva offers a range of road cycling routes, with a strong emphasis on challenging terrain. Out of 36 routes, 26 are classified as difficult, 9 as moderate, and 1 as easy. This means cyclists can find options from significant climbs to more rolling hills, often with excellent road surfaces.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Amieva?
A: The months of July and August are particularly popular for road cycling in Amieva. During this period, cyclists can generally expect favorable weather conditions to explore the diverse landscapes and challenging routes.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along road cycling routes in Amieva?
A: Road cycling in Amieva offers stunning views and passes through significant natural features. You'll often ride through or with views of the spectacular &lt;strong&gt;Picos de Europa National Park&lt;/strong&gt;, diverse forests, and the green Dobra valley. Notable attractions include the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6899851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagos de Covadonga&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/195967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ercina&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4350793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Enol&lt;/a&gt;. The ancient Arcediano Trail, a Roman road, also crosses parts of the region, adding a historical dimension to the natural landscapes.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes available in Amieva?
A: Yes, Amieva features several excellent circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067303053/collada-llomena-and-amieva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada Llomena and Amieva Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 50.3 km path with substantial elevation gain. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078480886/balconies-of-picos-de-europa-loop-from-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís&lt;/a&gt;, an 82.6 km trail offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Amieva?
A: While many routes in Amieva are challenging, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. However, the region is predominantly characterized by moderate to difficult terrain with significant elevation changes, making it more suitable for experienced cyclists or families with older, more capable riders.

Q: What is the quality of the road surfaces like for cycling in Amieva?
A: The quality of road surfaces in Amieva is generally excellent, with some sections having been recently paved. This provides a smooth and enjoyable riding experience, even on routes with challenging gradients.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists in Amieva?
A: Many road cycling routes in Amieva start from accessible points where parking is available. For example, the route 'Puerto de Casielles – round trip from the Área recreativa Trambesagües' suggests a starting point with recreational facilities, often implying parking availability. It's advisable to check specific route details for designated parking areas.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling in Amieva?
A: Yes, Amieva offers several options for long-distance road cycling. Routes can extend up to 115.1 km with over 2,200m of climbing. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14996201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Antolín de Bedón Beach – Holy Cave of Covadonga loop from Cuadonga / Covadonga&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 110 km.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Amieva?
A: The road cycling experience in Amieva is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the dramatic Picos de Europa mountains, the dense forests, scenic river valleys, and the varied terrain that offers options for all ability levels, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes in Amieva?
A: Road cycling routes in Amieva often pass through charming, traditional villages. While specific establishments are not listed, these villages typically offer opportunities for refreshments at local cafes or pubs, allowing cyclists to take a break and experience local culture.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Amieva region?
A: There are 36 road cycling routes available in the Amieva region, catering to various skill levels and preferences. This extensive network ensures a diverse range of options for exploring the area by bike.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the iconic Lakes of Covadonga?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067413747/trail-to-the-lakes-of-covadonga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Lakes of Covadonga&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that specifically offers significant climbs and views towards these iconic lakes. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/190299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrelagos Viewpoint (Lagos de Covadonga)&lt;/a&gt; in the vicinity.
